page.title: Low Back Pain — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Low Back Pain: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Low Back Pain: 130 alterations and 331 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Effects of breathing exercises on chronic low back pain.
Efficacy and safety of acupuncture for pregnancy-related low back pain.
High-intensity laser therapy in low back pain management.
The effectiveness of low-level laser therapy for nonspecific chronic low back pain.
Acupressure is an effective treatment for low back pain.
Acupuncture, acupressure and chiropractic may have a favourable effects on self-reported pain and functional limitations.
Auricular acupuncture is a promising practice for the treatment of chronic back pain in adults.
Classical and acupuncture massage may be of value in the reduction of low-back pain.
Effectiveness of ozone therapy compared to other therapies for low back pain.
Exercise prescription for improving chronic low back pain in adults.
Low-level laser therapy for chronic non-specific low back pain.
sit-stand workstations may reduce low back pain among workers.
Spa therapy can benefit pain reliving and improve lumbar spine function among patients with chronic low back pain.
Spinal manipulative therapy in older adults with chronic low back pain.
Tai Chi has an obvious effect of in relieving chronic low back pain.
The encouraging evidence of this study indicates that it is recommended to provide auricular acupressure to patients with chronic low back pain.
The effects of auricular acupressure on low back pain, neuropathy and sleep in patients with persistent spinal pain syndrome.
The present results indicate that regular exposure to odors increases pain thresholds in patients with chronic back pain.
A homeopathic complex with physiotherapy could significantly improve symptoms associated with chronic low-back pain due to osteoarthritis.
A homeopathic drug combination can improve the treatment of chronic low back pain.
Active-duty military personnel receiving chiropractic care exhibited improved strength and endurance.
Acupressure can be used as a drug-free and low-cost approach without side effects to improve fatigue in nurses with chronic back pain.
Acupressure on specific points was proven to reduce lower back pain in female nurses.
Acupuncture has immediate analgesic activity for acute lumbago.
Acupuncture offered significant effects for reducing or relieving lower back pain in pregnant women.
Acupuncture treatment is superior to anestheic injection for the treatment of low back pain.
Adding chiropractic manipulative therapy to standard medical care for patients with acute low back pain.
Association between psychosocial parameters and response to chiropractic care among older adults with chronic low back pain.
Bee venom acupuncture could improve bothersomeness, pain intensity, functional status, and the quality of life of patients with chronic low back pain.
Biological non-ablative repair of lumbar discs by transforaminal intradiscal laser irradiation: MRI quantitative analysis of the effects-preliminary report.
Both low and high-level laser therapy reduce pain and disability and improve lumbar mobility and quality of life in chronic low back pain patients.
Care outcomes for chiropractic outpatient veterans: a single-arm, pragmatic, pilot trial of multimodal chiropractic care for U.S. veterans with chronic low back pain.
Characteristics of chiropractic patients being treated for chronic low back and neck pain.
Chiropractic care, when added to usual medical care, resulted in moderate short-term improvements in low back pain intensity and disability.
Chiropractic manipulation has a therapeutic effect on pain relief for acute back pain and sciatica with disc protrusion.
Chiropractic spinal manipulative therapy leads to a reduction in benzodiazepine prescription in patients with radicular low back pain.
Clinical and functional evaluation of patients with acute low back pain and radiculopathy treated with different energy doses of low level laser therapy.
Combination of rehabilitative therapy with ultramicronized palmitoylethanolamide for chronic low back pain.
Comfrey root extract showed a remarkably potent and clinically relevant effect in reducing acute back pain.
Comparative study of combination therapy with non-steroidal anti inflammatory drugs and different doses of low level laser therapy in acute low back pain.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: ~90% of acute LBP episodes resolve within 6 weeks regardless of treatment; chronic LBP (>3 months) shows only 30-40% recovery at 1 year and high recurrence; opioid prescribing correlates inversely with recovery
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) + graded exercise: 50-70% sustained improvement at 1 year in chronic LBP; Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) — 66% pain-free or nearly pain-free at 1 year (JAMA Psychiatry 2021, vs 20% placebo); early active rehabilitation (vs bed rest) dramatically reduces chronification
remission.gap_size: Large for psychologically-informed approaches vs current opioid/surgical dominant model
remission.barrier_detail: Opioid prescribing for chronic LBP is associated with worse long-term outcomes yet remains extremely common; spinal fusion surgery rates 40x higher in US than UK despite no superiority over conservative care in most indications; active rehabilitation demonstrably superior to passive treatment but requires patient effort and provider time
remission.last_soc_change: 2017 — ACP guidelines explicitly recommended against opioids as first-line for chronic LBP; 2016 CDC opioid prescribing guidelines; imaging-guided approach deprioritized; exercise and CBT as first-line since 2017 — but implementation is poor
remission.outdated_treatments: Opioids as first-line or long-term treatment (CDC guidelines explicitly against this; long-term opioid therapy correlates with worse functional outcomes in LBP); routine lumbar spinal fusion for nonspecific chronic LBP (RCTs show no superiority over intensive rehabilitation); prolonged bed rest (explicitly contraindicated since 1990s, yet still advised by some practitioners); routine NSAID long-term use without reassessment
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) — 66% near-pain-free at 1 year; mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR) — RCT evidence; intradiscal biologics (platelet-rich plasma, stem cells — Phase 2 trials); spinal cord stimulation for failed back surgery syndrome
remission.frontier_signal: Pain Reprocessing Therapy (growing RCT base); resiniferatoxin (ultra-potent TRPV1 agonist causing ablation of pain-signaling neurons — Phase 1/2); biologic disc regeneration (GDF-5, TGF-beta intradiscal injection Phase 2); dorsal root ganglion stimulation (FDA approved 2016 for CRPS, expanding to LBP)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
